description: Empirically validate the monocentric city Bid-Rent Curve using San Francisco UrbanSim data in the notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/bid_rent_analysis.ipynb. Using the 'jobs', 'buildings', and 'parcels' tables from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5, identify the Central Business District (CBD) as the zone with the highest total jobs (linking jobs through buildings and parcels to zones) and establish the CBD point using the mean x and y coordinates of its parcels. Calculate the Euclidean distance for every parcel to the CBD and determine value intensity as the residential sales price per shape area. Generate a Bid-Rent Curve by grouping parcels into 1,000-unit distance bins and calculating the median value per sqft for each bin, saving the results to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/bid_rent_curve.csv. Identify the top 50 remote high-value anomalies (distance > 10,000 units) and save them to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/value_anomalies.csv. Save a visualization of the distance bins versus median value to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/bid_rent_chart.png. The notebook should be fully executed.
